race.name: The Millars Gran Fondo
race.display_name: The Millars UCI Gran Fondo World Series
race.tagline: Spain's only UCI Gran Fondo World Series qualifier through Sierra de Espadán Natural Park

vitals.location: Castellón de la Plana, Castellón Province, Spain
vitals.start_time: Gun-time start by age-group boxes (19-34, 35-39, etc.)
vitals.prize_purse: UCI medals for top 25% per age group; qualification to World Championships
vitals.aid_stations: Drink stations, nutrition stations, mechanical assistance, medical support
vitals.entry_fee: Not specified; includes Gobik jersey, finisher medal, UCI medal for qualifiers

climate.overview: March average 18-20°C; ideal early-season conditions in Spain's second most mountainous province[1][2]

terrain.overview: 158.9 km loop starting/finishing at Avenida del Mar, Castellón de la Plana; follows Mijares River through 28 municipalities and Sierra de Espadán Natural Park; two major climbs: Coll de la Ibola (17 km, 3% avg to 580m), Coll de la Lloma (10 km, 6-8% initial to 593m)[1][2][4]

logistics.explanation: The Millars Gran Fondo offers well-organized logistics with a dedicated expo, dorsal pickup, and recovery zone at the Recinto de Ferias y Mercados in Castellón de la Plana, including timed schedules and services like showers, but minor provisional changes deduct a point.[3][5]

length.explanation: The event features an impressive 157-159 km distance through 28 municipalities and the Sierra de Espadán, providing a full-day challenge ideal for Gran Fondo enthusiasts.[2][3][7]

technicality.explanation: While the route includes mythical climbs like Aín and Salto del Caballo in the Parque Natural de la Sierra de Espadán, it remains accessible on standard road bikes without extreme technical demands.[2][7]

elevation.explanation: With approximately 2,200 meters of positive elevation gain over its long distance, the climbs through Espadán offer substantial but manageable vertical challenge.[3][7]

climate.explanation: Held in March in Castellón Province, the Mediterranean climate ensures mild spring temperatures perfect for cycling, enhancing comfort during the event.[1][2]

altitude.explanation: The route stays at low to moderate altitudes in coastal and inland Castellón areas, rarely exceeding demanding high elevations in the Sierra de Espadán.[1][7]

adventure.explanation: Riding through the stunning Parque Natural de la Sierra de Espadán, picturesque villages like Aín, Fanzara, and Lucena del Cid, and along the Mijares River delivers an epic exploratory adventure.[1][2][4]

prestige.explanation: As Spain's sole UCI Gran Fondo World Series event, it qualifies top 25% age-group finishers for the UCI World Championships, though not yet a top global prestige draw.[1][2][3]

race_quality.explanation: High-quality organization shines with UCI medals, a Gobik maillot gift, pro ambassadors like Ivan Basso and Purito Rodríguez, and a neutralized 'tiempo de pistola' start.[1][3][4][7]

experience.explanation: Participants enjoy immersive scenery, international field, finisher medals, live music, and post-race festivities, creating an unforgettable Gran Fondo outing.[1][3][5]

community.explanation: Strong local support from Castellón, with involvement from sponsors like Quadis Marzá and a vibrant expo atmosphere fosters a welcoming cycling community vibe.[3][5]

field_depth.explanation: Attracts a diverse international peloton qualifying for UCI Worlds, but field depth is moderate as a newer Spanish event still building elite participation.[1][2][3]

value.explanation: At 65€ entry, riders get a premium Gobik maillot, UCI-classified racing, medals, and expo access, offering excellent value for a World Series qualifier.[2][3]

expenses.explanation: Beyond the reasonable 65€ fee, travel to Castellón de la Plana and accommodations in the second most mountainous Spanish province add moderate extra costs.[2][3]

biased_opinion.logistics: 4 - Valencia Airport 65km away; well-supported with mechanical assistance, parking, showers[1][2]
biased_opinion.length: 5 - True Gran Fondo distance at 158+ km[2][4]
biased_opinion.technicality: 3 - Road climbs with some steep sections but primarily steady gradients[4]
biased_opinion.elevation: 4 - 2,200+m over century distance provides solid challenge[2][4]
biased_opinion.adventure: 5 - Sierra de Espadán Natural Park, 28 municipalities, river valley beauty[1][2]
biased_opinion.prestige: 3 - UCI World Series qualifier, only one in Spain[1][3][6]
biased_opinion.race_quality: 4 - Professional organization, UCI-sanctioned, strong inaugural showing[1][5]
biased_opinion.experience: 5 - Gobik jersey gift, UCI medals, post-race meal, massages, qualifiers to Worlds[1][5]
biased_opinion.community: 4 - 1,400 riders from 27 nations in Year 1; age-group boxes[1][2]
biased_opinion.value: 4 - Jersey, medals, full support package for UCI qualifier[1][5]
biased_opinion.expenses: 3 - Spain travel reasonable but early-season flight costs variable
biased_opinion.cultural_impact: 3 - Establishes Spain in global Gran Fondo circuit; local pride in Castellón[2][5]

logistics.overview: Nearest airports: Valencia (VLC, 65km), Castellón-Costa Azahar (CDT). Start/finish Avenida del Mar, Castellón de la Plana. Full services: timing, expo, photos, video, meals, massages, mechanical support. UCI license required[1][2]

history.overview: Founded 2024 (inaugural March 10, Vila-Real); 1,400+ cyclists from 27 nations. 2024 winners: Lepoutre, de Vries; ambassadors Ivan Basso, Purito Rodriguez, Ibon Zugasti (category winner). Qualifiers advanced to Aalborg Worlds. 2025/2026 in Castellón de la Plana[1][2][4][5]

course_description.character: 158.9km / 2,213m from Castellón de la Plana via Mijares River valley. **Suffering Zones:** 1) Coll de la Ibola (km 27-44, 17km @ 3% to 580m) - long steady grind. 2) Coll de la Lloma (km 80+, 10km with 6-8% opening 5km to 593m) - race's steepest test. Fast descent to sea level finish[2][4]

non_negotiables: UCI cycling federation license with accident insurance
No time trial bikes; road/MTB/hybrid permitted (no tandems/recumbents)
Top 25% per 10 age groups (M/F) earn UCI medal + Worlds qualification
Gobik Challenge jersey included with registration

research_metadata.data_completeness: High - official distances/elevation confirmed; entry fee unspecified

youtube_data.search_query: The Millars Gran Fondo gran fondo cycling Castellón de la Plana

videos.title: 🔥 Millars UCI Gran Fondo de Castellón  con IBON ZUGASTI🚴💨
videos.description:
🚲 ¡Bienvenidos, DISFRUTONES DEL CICLISMO!

En este video os traigo una experiencia brutal en la Millars Gran Fondo de Castellón, una marcha cicloturista UCI con 158 km y 2.200 metros de desnivel. Pero lo mejor de todo… ¡compartiendo kilómetros con el bicharraco Ibon Zugasti! 🔥

Nos enfrentamos a los puertos de Aín y el Salto del Caballo, con rampas durísimas, paisajes espectaculares y un ritmo muy exigente ¿Cómo fue rodar junto a una leyenda del ciclismo? ¡Os lo cuento todo en el video!

⚠️ Bajadas con máxima precaución para disfrutar de la jornada con seguridad.

👦 Sobre mí: soy padre de tres hijas y ciclista desde hace 7 años. Me apasiona este deporte y trato de ayudar a quienes empiezan, porque sé lo duro que pueden ser los comienzos.

📲 Sígueme en mis redes sociales:
📸 Instagram: @fnz1986
🐦 Twitter: @FranMorcillo
🎵 TikTok: @fran.morcillo
🚴 Strava: Fran Morcillo

Si te gusta el contenido, ¡suscríbete y dale a like! 🚴‍♂️💨
videos.curation_reason: Recent first-person race experience video featuring the challenging 158km course with 2,200m elevation and key climbs including Aín and Salto del Caballo.

videos.title: Castellón Bike Events
videos.description:
Reunimos a organizadores de las siete pruebas de ciclismo en la provincia de Castellón que congregan a visitantes de todo el mundo en Emebikes.

Participaron: Publicom Sport Events, Gigante de Piedra, Desafio Maestrat, Mediterránea TRI, Castellón Gravel Race, Trixilxes, Millars UCI Gran Fondo y Sebastián Mora, ciclista profesional y actual campeón del mundo en pista.
